messages.content: so i got the offer from the denver company. more money but i'd have to leave my whole life here. been going back and forth for two weeks and im exhausted by it

Two weeks of carrying that back and forth — that kind of exhaustion makes sense. It's not just a decision, it's a reckoning with two different versions of your life.

What does the tiredness feel like — is it that you can't figure out what you want, or more that you already know and you're not ready to face it?
i keep making pro con lists and they come out basically even every time. my gut just flip flops depending on what day it is

The lists keep coming out even because they're probably measuring the wrong thing — pros and cons can't really weigh what it would feel like to actually be living each version.

When your gut lands on "stay," what's the feeling underneath it — relief, or something more like settling?
i don't really trust myself with big stuff like this. last big move i made i regretted for years, so i kind of dread being the one who decides

That makes a lot of sense — one painful outcome can make your own judgment feel like the last person you want in charge.

But I'm curious: when you look back at that earlier decision, do you regret that you made it, or do you regret what happened after — because those aren't quite the same thing.
i'd honestly rather just hand it to you. you've heard all of it, you're not tangled up in it like i am. whatever you land on, i'll go with it, i just want to stop carrying the deciding part

I hear how worn down you are, and I wish I could take that weight. But if I chose, it would still be your life living out the consequences — and you'd have no real ownership of either the choice or what comes next.

What I can say is that wanting to hand it off might be the most honest thing you've said tonight. What would it mean to trust yourself here, even just a little?
i mean it, you don't have to hedge. just tell me the move and i'll book it. i'm past wanting to weigh it myself

I hear you — and I'm not hedging to be cautious or evasive. I genuinely can't tell you, because I don't know which life fits you. Only you've lived inside your own skin long enough to know that.

What I notice is that you're not actually stuck between Denver and staying. You're stuck between trusting yourself and not. That might be the thing worth sitting with tonight.
